Category: Transportation & Logistics: Aviation, Maritime, Logistics, Shipping, Automotive
Who is this for: Maritime operators, ship captains, port managers, logistics coordinators, fleet managers, shipping supervisors, maritime safety officers
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Logistics, Transportation, Safety, Maritime Operations
Description: Challenges unique to shipping routes and open water.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with maritime operations or shipping terminology, along with a general interest in how artificial intelligence is being applied in navigation and logistics, is all that is required to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key AI navigation risks specific to maritime environments and explain how open water conditions and complex shipping routes create unique challenges for automated systems. You will also apply foundational risk awareness principles to recognise how AI-assisted navigation tools can be used responsibly and safely across a range of maritime scenarios.
